mai.notes: Most succinct (38 lines) but minimal: single in-range demo, no empty/<2-point guard, edge cases not demonstrated despite having the error path. Stray double blank line.

codex.notes: Best balance. Explicit <2-point guard, handles exact-endpoint matches, helpful range-error messages, lean and well-structured (59 lines).

claude.notes: Most complete and educational: docstrings, largest dataset, formatted table, demonstrates out-of-range and exact-match cases. But least succinct (~95 lines), over-engineered for a demo, lacks empty-list guard, and silently clamps out-of-range (debatable semantics for interpolation).

overall_rationale: codex strikes the best balance: correct with the most robust guards, complete enough to be genuinely demonstrative, and still concise. claude is the most educational but least succinct with questionable silent-clamp semantics. mai is the tightest but too thin to fully demonstrate the concept.
bias_disclosure: One implementation (claude) was the judge's own prior output; it was scored on the same criteria and did not win overall.
